translations.idiomatic: When Jiangbin died his son Chengde styled himself Han's grandson and shuttled to court constantly under Cheng and Ai; Han received him with unusual warmth.

sentences.zh: 東通尉犁六百五十里。

translations.literal: East connects to Weili six hundred fifty li.
translations.idiomatic: Weili lay six hundred fifty li to the east.

sentences.zh: 尉犁國，王治尉犁城，去長安六千七百五十里。

translations.literal: The state of Weili: the king rules from Weili city; it is six thousand seven hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Weili was ruled from Weili city, six thousand seven hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶千二百，口九千六百，勝兵二千人。

translations.literal: Households one thousand two hundred; population nine thousand six hundred; able soldiers two thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had twelve hundred households, ninety-six hundred people, and two thousand troops.

sentences.zh: 尉犁侯、安世侯、左右將、左右都尉、擊胡君各一人，譯長二人。

translations.literal: Weili marquis, Secure-the-Age marquis, left and right generals, left and right commandants, strike-Hu lord one each; interpreter chiefs two persons.
translations.idiomatic: Its officers included marquises of Weili and of Secure Age, paired generals and commandants, a strike-Hu lord, and two interpreter chiefs.

sentences.zh: 西至都護治所三百里，南與鄯善、且未接。

translations.literal: West to the Protector-General's seat three hundred li; south joins Shanshan and Qiemo.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General lay three hundred li west; Shanshan and Qiemo bordered it on the south.

sentences.zh: 危須國，王治危須城，去長安七千二百九十里。

translations.literal: The state of Weixu: the king rules from Weixu city; it is seven thousand two hundred ninety li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Weixu was governed from Weixu city, seven thousand two hundred ninety li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶七百，口四千九百，勝兵二千人。

translations.literal: Households seven hundred; population four thousand nine hundred; able soldiers two thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: It counted seven hundred households, forty-nine hundred inhabitants, and two thousand soldiers.

sentences.zh: 擊胡侯、擊胡都尉、左右將、左右都尉、左右騎君、擊胡君、譯長各一人。

translations.literal: Strike-Hu marquis, strike-Hu commandant, left and right generals, left and right commandants, left and right cavalry lords, strike-Hu lord, interpreter chief one each.
translations.idiomatic: It listed a strike-Hu marquis and commandant, paired generals and commandants, cavalry lords, a strike-Hu lord, and an interpreter chief—one of each.

sentences.zh: 西至都護治所五百里，至焉耆百里。

translations.literal: West to the Protector-General's seat five hundred li; to Yanqi one hundred li.
translations.idiomatic: Five hundred li west stood the Protector-General; Yanqi was a hundred li away.

sentences.zh: 焉耆國，王治員渠城，去長安七千三百里。

translations.literal: The state of Yanqi: the king rules from Yuanqu city; it is seven thousand three hundred li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The greater kingdom of Yanqi ruled from Yuanqu, seven thousand three hundred li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶四千，口三萬二千一百，勝兵六千人。

translations.literal: Households four thousand; population thirty-two thousand one hundred; able soldiers six thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had four thousand households, thirty-two thousand one hundred people, and six thousand warriors.

sentences.zh: 擊胡侯、卻胡侯、輔國侯、左右將、左右都尉、擊胡左右君、擊車師君、歸義車師君各一人，擊胡都尉、擊胡君各二人，譯長三人。

translations.literal: Strike-Hu marquis, repel-Hu marquis, state-assisting marquis, left and right generals, left and right commandants, strike-Hu left and right lords, strike-Jushi lord, submit-to-righteousness Jushi lord one each; strike-Hu commandants and strike-Hu lords two each; interpreter chiefs three persons.
translations.idiomatic: Officials included marquises for striking and repelling the Hu, an assistant marquis, paired generals and commandants, Hu-striking left and right lords, lords for striking and for submitting Jushi—one apiece where listed—two strike-Hu commandants and two strike-Hu lords, and three interpreter chiefs.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所四百里南至尉犁百里，北與烏孫接。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat four hundred li; south to Weili one hundred li; north joins Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: It lay four hundred li southwest of the Protector-General's headquarters, a hundred li south of Weili, and bordered Wusun on the north.

sentences.zh: 近海水多魚。

translations.literal: Near the sea water, many fish.
translations.idiomatic: It lay near brackish water rich in fish.

sentences.zh: 烏貪訾離國，王治於婁谷，去長安萬三百三十里。

translations.literal: The state of Wutan Zili: the king rules from Yulou valley; it is ten thousand three hundred thirty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Wutan Zili was ruled from Yulou valley, ten thousand three hundred thirty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶四十一，口二百三十一，勝兵五十七人。

translations.literal: Households forty-one; population two hundred thirty-one; able soldiers fifty-seven persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had only forty-one households, two hundred thirty-one people, and fifty-seven soldiers.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、左右都尉各一人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, left and right commandants one each.
translations.idiomatic: It had an assistant marquis and left and right commandants.

sentences.zh: 東與單桓、南與且彌、西與烏孫接。

translations.literal: East joins Danhuan; south joins Qiemi; west joins Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: It bordered Danhuan on the east, Qiemi on the south, and Wusun on the west.

sentences.zh: 卑陸國，王治天山東乾當國，去長安八千六百八十里。

translations.literal: The state of Beilu: the king rules east of Tianshan at Gandang state; it is eight thousand six hundred eighty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Beilu lay east of the Tianshan range at Gandang, eight thousand six hundred eighty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶二百二十七，口千三百八十七，勝兵四百二十二人。

translations.literal: Households two hundred twenty-seven; population one thousand three hundred eighty-seven; able soldiers four hundred twenty-two persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had two hundred twenty-seven households, thirteen hundred eighty-seven people, and four hundred twenty-two troops.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、左右將、左右都尉、左右譯長各一人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, left and right generals, left and right commandants, left and right interpreter chiefs one each.
translations.idiomatic: Its government included an assistant marquis, paired generals and commandants, and paired interpreter chiefs.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所千二百八十七里。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat one thousand two hundred eighty-seven li.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General's seat lay one thousand two hundred eighty-seven li to the southwest.

sentences.zh: 卑陸後國，王治番渠類谷，去長安八千七百一十里。

translations.literal: Beilu Rear state: the king rules at Fanqule valley; it is eight thousand seven hundred ten li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Rear Beilu was ruled from Fanqule valley, eight thousand seven hundred ten li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶四百六十二，口千一百三十七，勝兵三百五十人。

translations.literal: Households four hundred sixty-two; population one thousand one hundred thirty-seven; able soldiers three hundred fifty persons.
translations.idiomatic: It counted four hundred sixty-two households, eleven hundred thirty-seven inhabitants, and three hundred fifty soldiers.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、都尉、譯長各一人，將二人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, commandant, interpreter chief one each; generals two persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had an assistant marquis, a commandant, an interpreter chief, and two generals.

sentences.zh: 東與郁立師、北與匈奴、西與劫國、南與車師接。

translations.literal: East joins Yulishi; north joins the Xiongnu; west joins Jie; south joins Jushi.
translations.idiomatic: It bordered Yulishi on the east, the Xiongnu on the north, Jie on the west, and Jushi on the south.

sentences.zh: 郁立師國，王治內咄谷，去長安八千八百三十里。

translations.literal: The state of Yulishi: the king rules at Neidui valley; it is eight thousand eight hundred thirty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Yulishi was governed from Neidui valley, eight thousand eight hundred thirty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶百九十，口千四百四十五，勝兵三百三十一人。

translations.literal: Households one hundred ninety; population one thousand four hundred forty-five; able soldiers three hundred thirty-one persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had one hundred ninety households, fourteen hundred forty-five people, and three hundred thirty-one troops.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、左右都尉、譯長各一人，東與車師後城長、西與卑陸、北與匈奴接。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, left and right commandants, interpreter chief one each; east joins Jushi rear-city chief; west joins Beilu; north joins the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Officers included an assistant marquis, left and right commandants, and an interpreter chief; on the east it bordered the commandant of Jushi's rear city, on the west Beilu, and on the north the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 單桓國，王治單桓城，去長安八千八百七十里。

translations.literal: The state of Danhuan: the king rules from Danhuan city; it is eight thousand eight hundred seventy li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Danhuan was ruled from Danhuan city, eight thousand eight hundred seventy li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶二十七，口百九十四，勝兵四十五人。

translations.literal: Households twenty-seven; population one hundred ninety-four; able soldiers forty-five persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had twenty-seven households, one hundred ninety-four people, and forty-five soldiers.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、將、左右都尉、譯長各一人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, general, left and right commandants, interpreter chief one each.
translations.idiomatic: It listed an assistant marquis, a general, left and right commandants, and an interpreter chief.

sentences.zh: 蒲類國，王治天山西疏榆谷，去長安八千三百六十里。

translations.literal: The state of Pulei: the king rules west of Tianshan at Shuyu valley; it is eight thousand three hundred sixty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Pulei lay west of the Tianshan in Shuyu valley, eight thousand three hundred sixty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶三百二十五，口二千三十二，勝兵七百九十九人。

translations.literal: Households three hundred twenty-five; population two thousand thirty-two; able soldiers seven hundred ninety-nine persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had three hundred twenty-five households, two thousand thirty-two inhabitants, and seven hundred ninety-nine warriors.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、左右將、左右都尉各一人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, left and right generals, left and right commandants one each.
translations.idiomatic: It had an assistant marquis and paired generals and commandants.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所千三百八十七里。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat one thousand three hundred eighty-seven li.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General lay one thousand three hundred eighty-seven li to the southwest.

sentences.zh: 蒲類後國，王去長安八千六百三十國。

translations.literal: Pulei Rear state: the king is eight thousand six hundred thirty states from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Rear Pulei: the king's seat lay eight thousand six hundred thirty li from Chang'an; the text mistakenly writes guo instead of li.

sentences.zh: 戶百，口千七十，勝兵三百三十四人，輔國侯、將、左右都尉、譯長各一人。

translations.literal: Households one hundred; population one thousand seventy; able soldiers three hundred thirty-four persons; state-assisting marquis, general, left and right commandants, interpreter chief one each.
translations.idiomatic: It had a hundred households, ten hundred seventy people, three hundred thirty-four troops, and one each of assistant marquis, general, commandants, and interpreter chief.

sentences.zh: 西且彌國，王治天山東於大谷，去長安八千六百七十里。

translations.literal: West Qiemo state: the king rules east of Tianshan at Yudai valley; it is eight thousand six hundred seventy li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: West Qiemo lay east of the Tianshan in Yudai valley, eight thousand six hundred seventy li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶三百三十二，口千九百二十六，勝兵七百三十八人。

translations.literal: Households three hundred thirty-two; population one thousand nine hundred twenty-six; able soldiers seven hundred thirty-eight persons.
translations.idiomatic: It counted three hundred thirty-two households, nineteen hundred twenty-six people, and seven hundred thirty-eight soldiers.

sentences.zh: 西且彌侯、左右將、左右騎君各一人。

translations.literal: West Qiemo marquis, left and right generals, left and right cavalry lords one each.
translations.idiomatic: Its titles included the West Qiemo marquis with paired generals and cavalry lords.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所千四百八十七里。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat one thousand four hundred eighty-seven li.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General stood one thousand four hundred eighty-seven li to the southwest.

sentences.zh: 東且彌國，王治天山東兌虛谷，去長安八千二百五十里。

translations.literal: East Qiemo state: the king rules east of Tianshan at Duixu valley; it is eight thousand two hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: East Qiemo was ruled from Duixu valley east of the Tianshan, eight thousand two hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶百九十一，口千九百四十八，勝兵五百七十二人。

translations.literal: Households one hundred ninety-one; population one thousand nine hundred forty-eight; able soldiers five hundred seventy-two persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had one hundred ninety-one households, nineteen hundred forty-eight inhabitants, and five hundred seventy-two troops.

sentences.zh: 東且彌侯、左右都尉各一人。

translations.literal: East Qiemo marquis, left and right commandants one each.
translations.idiomatic: It listed the East Qiemo marquis with left and right commandants.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所千五百八十七里。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat one thousand five hundred eighty-seven li.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General's headquarters lay one thousand five hundred eighty-seven li southwest.

sentences.zh: 劫國，王治天山東丹渠谷，去長安八千五百七十里。

translations.literal: The state of Jie: the king rules east of Tianshan at Danqu valley; it is eight thousand five hundred seventy li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Jie lay east of the Tianshan in Danqu valley, eight thousand five hundred seventy li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶九十九，口五百，勝兵百一十五人。

translations.literal: Households ninety-nine; population five hundred; able soldiers one hundred fifteen persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had ninety-nine households, five hundred people, and one hundred fifteen soldiers.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、都尉、譯長各一人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, commandant, interpreter chief one each.
translations.idiomatic: It had an assistant marquis, a commandant, and an interpreter chief.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所千四百八十七里。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat one thousand four hundred eighty-seven li.
translations.idiomatic: One thousand four hundred eighty-seven li southwest stood the Protector-General.

sentences.zh: 狐胡國，王治車師柳谷，去長安八千二百里。

translations.literal: The state of Huhu: the king rules at Jushi Liugu; it is eight thousand two hundred li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Huhu was governed from Jushi's Liugu, eight thousand two hundred li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶五十五，口二百六十四，勝兵四十五人。

translations.literal: Households fifty-five; population two hundred sixty-four; able soldiers forty-five persons.
translations.idiomatic: It counted fifty-five households, two hundred sixty-four inhabitants, and forty-five troops.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、左右都尉各一人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, left and right commandants one each.
translations.idiomatic: It had an assistant marquis and paired commandants.

sentences.zh: 西至都護治所千一百四十七里，至焉耆七百七十里。

translations.literal: West to the Protector-General's seat one thousand one hundred forty-seven li; to Yanqi seven hundred seventy li.
translations.idiomatic: Eleven hundred forty-seven li west lay the Protector-General; Yanqi was seven hundred seventy li away.

sentences.zh: 山國，王去長安七千一百七十里。

translations.literal: The state of Shan: the king is seven thousand one hundred seventy li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The hill country of Shan lay seven thousand one hundred seventy li from the capital.

sentences.zh: 戶四百五十，口五千，勝兵千人。

translations.literal: Households four hundred fifty; population five thousand; able soldiers one thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had four hundred fifty households, five thousand people, and a thousand soldiers.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、左右將、左右都尉、譯長各一人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, left and right generals, left and right commandants, interpreter chief one each.
translations.idiomatic: Officers included an assistant marquis, paired generals and commandants, and an interpreter chief.

sentences.zh: 西至尉犁二百四十里，西北至焉耆百六十里，西至危須二百六十里，東南與鄯善、且末接。

translations.literal: West to Yanqi two hundred forty li; northwest to Yanqi one hundred sixty li; west to Weixu two hundred sixty li; southeast joins Shanshan and Qiemo.
translations.idiomatic: Two hundred forty li west was Weili, one hundred sixty li northwest was Yanqi, two hundred sixty li west was Weixu, and Shanshan and Qiemo lay to the southeast.

sentences.zh: 山出鐵，民出居，寄田糴谷於焉耆、危須。

translations.literal: The mountains produce iron; the people go out to dwell, rent fields and buy grain from Yanqi and Weixu.
translations.idiomatic: Its hills yielded iron; the people lived abroad, leasing fields and buying grain in Yanqi and Weixu.

sentences.zh: 車師前國，王治交河城。

translations.literal: Jushi Front state: the king rules from Jiaohe city.
translations.idiomatic: Front Jushi was ruled from Jiaohe.

sentences.zh: 河水分流繞城下，故號交河。

translations.literal: River waters divide flow and circle below the city; therefore it is called Jiaohe.
translations.idiomatic: Two branches of a river meet beneath the walls, which gives Jiaohe its name, Confluence.

sentences.zh: 去長安八千一百五十里。

translations.literal: It is eight thousand one hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The city stood eight thousand one hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶七百，口六千五十，勝兵千八百六十五人。

translations.literal: Households seven hundred; population six thousand fifty; able soldiers one thousand eight hundred sixty-five persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had seven hundred households, six thousand fifty inhabitants, and one thousand eight hundred sixty-five warriors.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、安國侯、左右將、都尉、歸漢都尉、車師君、通善君、鄉善君各一人，譯長二人。

translations.literal: State-assisting marquis, state-securing marquis, left and right generals, commandant, submit-to-Han commandant, Jushi lord, Connect-with-Good lord, Toward-Good lord one each; interpreter chiefs two persons.
translations.idiomatic: Its bureaucracy paired assistant and securing marquises, generals, commandants, a submit-to-Han commandant, lords of Jushi, of connecting goodness, and of inclining to goodness, plus two interpreter chiefs.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所千八百七里，至焉耆八百三十五里。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat one thousand eight hundred seven li; to Yanqi eight hundred thirty-five li.
translations.idiomatic: Eighteen hundred seven li southwest stood the Protector-General; Yanqi was eight hundred thirty-five li off.

sentences.zh: 車師後國，王治務塗谷，去長安八千九百五十里。

translations.literal: Jushi Rear state: the king rules at Wutu valley; it is eight thousand nine hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Rear Jushi ruled from Wutu valley, eight thousand nine hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶五百九十五，口四千七百七十四，勝兵千八百九十人。

translations.literal: Households five hundred ninety-five; population four thousand seven hundred seventy-four; able soldiers one thousand eight hundred ninety persons.
translations.idiomatic: It counted five hundred ninety-five households, forty-seven hundred seventy-four people, and one thousand eight hundred ninety troops.

sentences.zh: 擊胡侯、左右將、左右都尉、道民君、譯長各一人。

translations.literal: Strike-Hu marquis, left and right generals, left and right commandants, Guide-the-People lord, interpreter chief one each.
translations.idiomatic: Officers included a strike-Hu marquis, paired generals and commandants, a guide-the-people lord, and an interpreter chief.

sentences.zh: 西南至都護治所千二百三十七里。

translations.literal: Southwest to the Protector-General's seat one thousand two hundred thirty-seven li.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General lay one thousand two hundred thirty-seven li to the southwest.

sentences.zh: 車師都尉國，戶四十，口三百三十三，勝兵八十四人。

translations.literal: Jushi Commandant state: households forty; population three hundred thirty-three; able soldiers eighty-four persons.
translations.idiomatic: The commandant's canton of Jushi had forty households, three hundred thirty-three people, and eighty-four troops.

sentences.zh: 車師後城長國，戶百五十四，口九百六十，勝兵二百六十人。

translations.literal: Jushi Rear City Chief state: households one hundred fifty-four; population nine hundred sixty; able soldiers two hundred sixty persons.
translations.idiomatic: The rear-city chief's canton counted one hundred fifty-four households, nine hundred sixty inhabitants, and two hundred sixty soldiers.

sentences.zh: 武帝天漢二年，以匈奴降者介和王為開陵侯，將樓蘭國兵始擊車師，匈奴遣右賢王將數萬騎救之，漢兵不利，引去。

translations.literal: Emperor Wu's Tianhan second year, taking Xiongnu surrenderer King Jiehe as Marquis of Kaifeng-ling, leading Loulan state's troops first struck Jushi; the Xiongnu dispatched the Right Worthy King leading several tens of thousands of cavalry to save them; Han troops were not favorable, drew away.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Tianhan, Emperor Wu made the Xiongnu defector King Jiehe marquis of Kaifeng-ling and sent Loulan troops in the first strike on Jushi; the Right Worthy King rode to the rescue with tens of thousands of horse, Han faltered, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 征和四年，遣重合侯馬通將四萬騎擊匈奴，道過車師北，復遣開陵侯將樓蘭、尉犁、危須凡六國兵別擊車師，勿令得遮重合侯。

translations.literal: Zhenghe fourth year, dispatched Marquis of Chonghe Ma Tong leading forty thousand cavalry to strike the Xiongnu, the road passing north of Jushi; again dispatched Marquis of Kaifeng-ling leading Loulan, Weili, Weixu in all six states' troops separately to strike Jushi, not letting them obtain blocking Chonghe.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Zhenghe, Marquis Chonghe Ma Tong took forty thousand horse against the Xiongnu along a route north of Jushi, while Marquis Kaifeng-ling was sent with troops from six states including Loulan, Weili, and Weixu to hit Jushi and keep it from intercepting Chonghe.

sentences.zh: 諸國兵共圍車師，車師王降服，臣屬漢。

translations.literal: The various states' troops together surrounded Jushi; the Jushi king submitted and served, became subject to Han.
translations.idiomatic: The allied hosts besieged Jushi until its king yielded and acknowledged Han.

sentences.zh: 昭帝時，匈奴復使四千騎田車師。

translations.literal: In Emperor Zhao's time, the Xiongnu again dispatched four thousand cavalry to field-cultivate Jushi.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Zhao the Xiongnu again sent four thousand riders to farm Jushi.

sentences.zh: 宣帝即位，遣五將將兵擊匈奴，車師田者驚去，車師復通於漢。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xuan took the throne, dispatched five generals leading troops to strike the Xiongnu; Jushi fielders were frightened and left; Jushi again opened communication with Han.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan's first campaign against the Xiongnu scattered the Jushi colonists, and Jushi reopened ties with Han.

sentences.zh: 匈奴怒，召其太子軍宿，欲以為質。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu were angry, summoned their crown prince Junxiu, wished to make him hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Enraged, the Xiongnu summoned crown prince Junxiu to hold him hostage.

sentences.zh: 軍宿，焉耆外孫，不欲質匈奴，亡走焉耆。

translations.literal: Junxiu, Yanqi's maternal grandson, did not wish to be hostage to the Xiongnu; fled and ran to Yanqi.
translations.idiomatic: Junxiu, a grandson of Yanqi on his mother's side, refused to be a Xiongnu hostage and fled to Yanqi.

sentences.zh: 車師王更立子烏貴為太子。

translations.literal: The Jushi king changed and established son Wugui as crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Jushi replaced him with his son Wugui as heir.

sentences.zh: 及烏貴立為王，與匈奴結婚姻，教匈奴遮漢道通烏孫者。

translations.literal: When Wugui was established as king, he bound marriage with the Xiongnu and taught the Xiongnu to block the Han road that communicated with Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: Once Wugui ruled he married into the Xiongnu and showed them how to cut Han traffic to Wusun.

sentences.zh: 地節二年，漢遣侍郎鄭吉、校尉司馬喜將免刑罪人田渠犁，積穀，欲以攻車師。

translations.literal: Dijie second year, Han dispatched Palace Gentleman Zheng Ji, Colonel Sima Xi leading amnestied convicts to field Quli, storing grain, wishing thereby to attack Jushi.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Dijie, Zheng Ji and Colonel Sima Xi led commuted convicts to farm Quli and pile grain for an attack on Jushi.

sentences.zh: 至秋收谷，吉、喜發城郭諸國兵萬餘人，自與所將田士千五百人共擊車師，攻交河城，破之。

translations.literal: When autumn harvest gathered grain, Ji and Xi raised walled-city states' troops more than ten thousand persons, themselves together with the field soldiers one thousand five hundred they led jointly struck Jushi, attacked Jiaohe city, broke it.
translations.idiomatic: After the autumn harvest they mustered over ten thousand oasis troops and, with their own fifteen hundred colonists, stormed Jushi, took Jiaohe, and broke the defenses.

sentences.zh: 王尚在其北石城中，未得，會軍食盡，吉等且罷兵，歸渠犁田。

translations.literal: The king was still in his north stone city, not obtained; meeting army food exhausted, Ji and others for the moment ceased troops, returned to Quli to field.
translations.idiomatic: The king held out in his northern stone fort; supplies ran out, so Zheng Ji broke off the siege and brought the men back to farm at Quli.

sentences.zh: 收秋畢，復發兵攻車師王於石城。

translations.literal: After gathering the autumn harvest complete, again raised troops to attack the Jushi king at stone city.
translations.idiomatic: When the harvest was in they marched again on the king in the stone fort.

sentences.zh: 王聞漢兵且至，北走匈奴求救，匈奴未為發兵。

translations.literal: The king heard Han troops were about to arrive, fled north to the Xiongnu seeking rescue; the Xiongnu did not for him dispatch troops.
translations.idiomatic: The king fled north for Xiongnu aid, but none came.

sentences.zh: 王來還，與貴人蘇猶議欲降漢，恐不見信。

translations.literal: The king came back, with noble Suyou deliberated wishing to surrender to Han, feared not being believed.
translations.idiomatic: He returned and plotted with the noble Suyou to submit to Han, yet feared Han would not trust him.

sentences.zh: 蘇猶教王擊匈奴邊國小蒲類，斬首，略其人民，以降吉。

translations.literal: Suyou taught the king to strike the Xiongnu border state Little Pulei, behead, plunder its people, thereby to submit to Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Suyou urged him to raid the Xiongnu vassal state of Lesser Pulei, take heads and captives, and offer them to Zheng Ji as proof of good faith.

sentences.zh: 車師旁小金附國隨漢軍後盜車師，車師王復自請擊破金附。

translations.literal: Beside Jushi a small Jinfu state followed Han army's rear and robbed Jushi; the Jushi king again himself requested to strike and break Jinfu.
translations.idiomatic: A petty state called Jinfu nipped at Han's rear and raided Jushi, so the king asked leave to crush Jinfu himself.

sentences.zh: 匈奴聞車師降漢，發兵攻車師，吉、喜引兵北逢之，匈奴不敢前。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu heard Jushi had surrendered to Han, raised troops to attack Jushi; Ji and Xi led troops north met them; the Xiongnu did not dare advance.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xiongnu struck Jushi after its submission, Zheng Ji and Sima Xi marched north to meet them and the Xiongnu halted.

sentences.zh: 吉、喜即留一候與卒二十人留守王，吉等引兵歸渠犁。

translations.literal: Ji and Xi thereupon left one hou with twenty soldiers to guard the king; Ji and others led troops back to Quli.
translations.idiomatic: They left one signal officer and twenty men with the king while the main body withdrew to Quli.

sentences.zh: 車師王恐匈奴兵復至而見殺也，乃輕騎奔烏孫，吉即迎其妻子置渠犁。

translations.literal: The Jushi king feared Xiongnu troops would again arrive and he would be killed; thereupon light cavalry fled to Wusun; Ji then welcomed his wife and children and placed them at Quli.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing the Xiongnu would kill him, the king bolted on light horse to Wusun; Zheng Ji brought his family to safety at Quli.

sentences.zh: 東奏事，至酒泉，有詔還田渠犁及車師，益積穀以安西國，侵匈奴。

translations.literal: Eastward memorializing affairs, reaching Jiuquan, there was an edict ordering return to field Quli and Jushi, increase stored grain to pacify western states, encroach the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: He was eastbound to report when orders caught him at Jiuquan: resume farming at Quli and Jushi, pile grain to steady the west, and pressure the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 吉還，傳送車師王妻子詣長安，賞賜甚厚，每朝會四夷，常尊顯以示之。

translations.literal: Ji returned, escorted the Jushi king's wife and children to Chang'an; rewards were very thick; whenever court assemblies of the four Yi, constantly honored and displayed them to show them.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Ji convoyed the royal family to Chang'an, where they were richly rewarded and paraded at audiences with the frontier peoples.

sentences.zh: 於是吉始使吏卒三百人別田車師。

translations.literal: Thereupon Ji first dispatched officials and soldiers three hundred persons separately to field Jushi.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Ji then posted three hundred clerks and troops to colonize Jushi.

sentences.zh: 得降者，言單于大臣皆曰：「車師地肥美，近匈奴，使漢得之，多田積穀，必害人國，不可不爭也。

translations.literal: Obtaining a deserter, who said the Chanyu's great ministers all said: 'Jushi land is fat and beautiful, near the Xiongnu; if Han obtains it, much fields stored grain, will surely harm our state—cannot but contest it.'
translations.idiomatic: A turncoat reported the chanyu's council saying, 'Jushi is rich pasture hard by us; if Han farms and stores grain there, our realm suffers—we must fight for it.'

sentences.zh: 」果遣騎來擊田者，吉乃與校尉盡將渠犁田士千五百人往田，匈奴復益遣騎來，漢田卒少不能當，保車師城中。

translations.literal: Truly dispatched cavalry to come strike the fielders; Ji then together with the colonel led all Quli field soldiers one thousand five hundred persons to go field; the Xiongnu again increased dispatching cavalry to come; Han field soldiers were few and could not withstand, protected themselves in Jushi city.
translations.idiomatic: They sent horse against the colonists, so Zheng Ji and the colonel marched every Quli farmer—fifteen hundred men—into Jushi; the Xiongnu poured in more riders, and the outnumbered Han shut themselves in the city.

sentences.zh: 匈奴將即其城下謂吉曰：「單于必爭此地，不可田也。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu general beneath their city said to Ji: 'The Chanyu must contest this land; you cannot field it.'
translations.idiomatic: A Xiongnu general rode under the walls and told Zheng Ji, 'The chanyu claims this soil—no plowing for you.'

sentences.zh: 」圍城數日乃解。

translations.literal: Surrounded the city several days then released.
translations.idiomatic: They besieged the town for days, then lifted the siege.

sentences.zh: 後常數千騎往來守車師，吉上書言：「車師去渠犁千餘里，間以河山，北近匈奴，漢兵在渠犁者勢不能相救，願益田卒。

translations.literal: Afterward constantly several thousand cavalry went back and forth guarding Jushi; Ji submitted a memorial saying: 'Jushi is more than one thousand li from Quli, separated by mountains and rivers, north near the Xiongnu; Han troops at Quli in situation cannot mutually rescue; wish to increase field soldiers.'
translations.idiomatic: Thousands of Xiongnu horse then ringed Jushi; Zheng Ji wrote that the city lay a thousand li from Quli across rough terrain under Xiongnu guns, so the Quli garrison could not relieve it and begged for more colonists.

sentences.zh: 」公卿議以為道遠煩費，可且罷車師田者。

translations.literal: The dukes and ministers deliberated, thought the road distant vexing expense, could for the moment cease Jushi fielders.
translations.idiomatic: The high ministers judged the route too costly and voted to suspend the Jushi colony.

sentences.zh: 詔遣長羅侯將張掖、酒泉騎出車師北千餘里，揚威武車師旁。

translations.literal: Edict dispatched Marquis of Changluo leading Zhangye and Jiuquan cavalry out north of Jushi more than one thousand li, displaying martial awe beside Jushi.
translations.idiomatic: An edict sent Marquis Changluo with Zhangye and Jiuquan cavalry a thousand li north of Jushi to overawe the nomads.

sentences.zh: 胡騎引去，吉乃得出，歸渠犁，凡三校尉屯田。

translations.literal: Hu cavalry drew away; Ji then was able to get out, returned to Quli; in all three colonels garrison-fielded.
translations.idiomatic: The Hu horse withdrew and Zheng Ji escaped back to Quli, where three colonels resumed the farms.

sentences.zh: 車師王之走烏孫也，烏孫留不遣，遣使上書，願留車師王，備國有急，可從西道以擊匈奴。

translations.literal: When the Jushi king fled to Wusun, Wusun detained and would not send him, dispatched envoys submitting a memorial, wished to detain the Jushi king, if the state had emergency, could from the west road strike the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: When the king of Jushi fled to Wusun, that kingdom held him and memorialized to keep him as a hostage who, in a crisis, could strike the Xiongnu from the west.

sentences.zh: 漢許之。

translations.literal: Han permitted it.
translations.idiomatic: Han agreed.

sentences.zh: 於是漢召故車師太子軍宿在焉耆者，立以為王，盡徙車師國民令居渠犁，遂以車師故地與匈奴。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han summoned the former Jushi crown prince Junxiu who was at Yanqi, established him as king, entirely moved Jushi state's people ordering them to dwell at Quli, thereupon gave Jushi's old lands to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Han then called forward the old crown prince Junxiu from Yanqi, set him on the throne, moved the whole nation of Jushi to Quli, and ceded old Jushi to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 車師王得近漢田官，與匈奴絕，亦安樂親漢。

translations.literal: The Jushi king obtained nearness to Han field officials, cut off from the Xiongnu, also peaceful happy and close to Han.
translations.idiomatic: The new king lived beside Han's farm officers, cut off from the Xiongnu, and settled happily under Han influence.

sentences.zh: 後漢使侍郎殷廣德責烏孫，求車師王烏貴，將詣闕，賜第與其妻子居。

translations.literal: Afterward Han sent Palace Gentleman Yin Guangde to reproach Wusun, seeking Jushi king Wugui, led him to the palace gates, gifted a mansion for him and wife and children to dwell.
translations.idiomatic: Later Han sent Gentleman Yin Guangde to rebuke Wusun and demand King Wugui, who was brought to the capital, given a house, and lodged with his family.

sentences.zh: 是歲，元康四年也。

translations.literal: This year was Yuankang fourth year.
translations.idiomatic: The year was the fourth of Yuankang.

sentences.zh: 其後置戍己校尉屯田，居車師故地。

translations.literal: Afterward established Wu and Ji colonels to garrison-field, dwelling in Jushi's old lands.
translations.idiomatic: Later Wu and Ji colonels were posted to farm the old territory of Jushi.

sentences.zh: 元始中，車師後王國有新道，出五船北，通玉門關，往來差近，戊己校尉徐普欲開以省道裡半，避白龍堆之厄。

translations.literal: In Yuanshi period, Jushi Rear kingdom had a new road, going out north of Wuchuan, connecting Jade Gate Pass, comings and goings somewhat nearer; Wu-Ji Colonel Xu Pu wished to open it to save road mileage by half, avoiding the hardship of White Dragon Mound.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yuanshi the rear kingdom of Jushi opened a new track north of Wuchuan to the Jade Gate, shortening the journey and skirting the White Dragon Desert; Colonel Xu Pu of Wu and Ji wished to formalize it.

sentences.zh: 車師後王姑句以道當為拄置，心不便也。

translations.literal: Jushi Rear King Guju thought the road would be set as a post obstruction, in heart not convenient.
translations.idiomatic: Rear king Guju saw the route as a choke point that would harm his interests.

sentences.zh: 地又頗與匈奴南將軍地接，曾欲分明其界然後奏之，召姑句使證之，不肯，系之。

translations.literal: The land also rather connected with the Xiongnu South General's land; once wished to clarify its boundaries then memorialize; summoned Guju to send him to testify; he refused; bound him.
translations.idiomatic: His territory marched with the Xiongnu south general's zone; the colonel meant to fix the line and summoned Guju to testify, but he refused and was jailed.

sentences.zh: 姑句數以牛羊賕吏，求出不得。

translations.literal: Guju several times used cattle and sheep to bribe clerks, sought release but could not obtain.
translations.idiomatic: Guju tried to buy his freedom with livestock but failed.

sentences.zh: 姑句家矛端生火，其妻股紫陬謂姑句曰：「矛端生火，此兵氣也，利以用兵。

translations.literal: At Guju's home a fire sprang on the spear point; his wife Guzizhou said to Guju: 'Fire on the spear point—this is martial qi; profitable to use arms.
translations.idiomatic: A flame danced on his spearhead at home; his wife Zizhou said, 'Fire on a spear is the sign of war—time to fight.

sentences.zh: 前車師前王為都護司馬所殺，今久系必死，不如降匈奴。

translations.literal: Formerly the Jushi Front king was killed by the Protector-General's major; now long bound must die; not as good as surrender to the Xiongnu.'
translations.idiomatic: The front king of Jushi died at a Protector-General's major's hand; if you stay chained you die—better to flee to the Xiongnu.'

sentences.zh: 」即馳突出高昌壁，入匈奴。

translations.literal: Thereupon galloping burst out Gaochang rampart, entered the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: He broke out of Gaochang fort and rode to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 又去胡來王唐兜，國比大種赤水羌，數相冠，不勝，告急都護。

translations.literal: Also the king of Hulai Tangdou, whose state bordered the great tribe Chishui Qiang, several times crowned each other, could not overcome, urgently reported to the Protector-General.
translations.idiomatic: King Tangdou of Hulai, whose realm bordered the great Chishui Qiang, had clashed repeatedly, lost, and begged the Protector-General for aid.

sentences.zh: 都護但欽不以時救助，唐兜困急，怨欽，東守玉門關。

translations.literal: Protector-General Dan Qin did not in time rescue; Tangdou, trapped and urgent, resented Qin, eastward guarded Jade Gate Pass.
translations.idiomatic: Dan Qin failed to help in time; Tangdou, desperate, blamed Qin and blocked the Jade Gate from within.

sentences.zh: 玉門關不內，即將妻子人民千餘人亡降匈奴。

translations.literal: Jade Gate Pass would not admit him; thereupon leading wife, children, and people more than one thousand persons fled and surrendered to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: When the pass refused him entry, he led over a thousand followers with families and defected to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 匈奴受之，而遣使上書言狀。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu accepted them, and dispatched envoys upward submitting a memorial stating the situation.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu took them in and reported the matter to the court.

sentences.zh: 是時，新都侯王莽秉政，遣中郎將王昌等使匈奴，告單于西域內屬，不當得受。

translations.literal: At this time Marquis of Xindu Wang Mang grasped government, dispatched Palace Gentlemen Wang Chang and others as envoys to the Xiongnu, telling the Chanyu that the Western Regions were inner subjects and ought not to receive them.
translations.idiomatic: While Wang Mang held power he sent Wang Chang and others to tell the chanyu that the Western Regions belonged to Han and must not shelter the fugitives.

sentences.zh: 單于謝屬。

translations.literal: The Chanyu apologized and yielded them.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu apologized and handed them over.

sentences.zh: 執二王以付使者。

translations.literal: Seized the two kings and handed them to the envoys.
translations.idiomatic: He seized both kings and delivered them to Han envoys.

sentences.zh: 莽使中郎王萌待西域惡都奴界上逢受。

translations.literal: Mang ordered Palace Gentleman Wang Meng to wait on the Western Regions Wudunu border to meet and receive.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang told Gentleman Wang Meng to meet the party on the frontier at Wudunu.

sentences.zh: 單于遣使送，因請其罪。

translations.literal: The Chanyu dispatched envoys to escort them, thereby requesting pardon for their crime.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu sent escorts and asked forgiveness for his part.

sentences.zh: 使者以聞，莽不聽，詔下會西域諸國王，陳軍斬姑句、唐兜以示之。

translations.literal: The envoys reported hearing; Mang did not listen, edict sent down to assemble the Western Regions' kings, arrayed troops beheaded Guju and Tangdou to display to them.
translations.idiomatic: The envoys reported; Mang ignored the plea, summoned the kings of the west, and executed Guju and Tangdou before the host as a lesson.

sentences.zh: 至莽篡位，建國二年，以廣新公甄豐為右伯，當出西域。

translations.literal: Reaching Mang's usurpation, Jian'guo second year, taking Marquis of Guangxin Zhen Feng as Right Overlord, about to go out to the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: When Mang seized the throne, in the second year of Jian'guo he named Zhen Feng of Guangxin Right Overlord for a mission west.

sentences.zh: 車師後王須置離聞之，與其右將股鞮、左將屍泥支謀曰：「聞甄公為西域太伯，當出，故事給使者牛、羊、谷、芻茭，導譯，前五威將過，所給使尚未能備。

translations.literal: Jushi Rear King Xuzhili heard it, together with his right general Yindi and left general Shinizhi plotted, saying: 'We hear Lord Zhen is Great Overlord of the Western Regions, about to go out; by old story one supplies envoys cattle, sheep, grain, fodder, guides and interpreters; the previous Five Might generals passed—what was supplied to them still could not be fully prepared.
translations.idiomatic: Rear king Xuzhili of Jushi heard the news and conspired with his generals Yindi and Shinizhi: 'They say Zhen Feng will be supreme steward of the west; by custom we owe envoys oxen, sheep, grain, fodder, and guides, yet the last Five Might mission bled us dry.

sentences.zh: 今太伯復出，國益貧，恐不能稱。

translations.literal: Now the Great Overlord again goes out; the state grows poorer; fear cannot meet the measure.'
translations.idiomatic: If this great steward comes again our poor kingdom cannot possibly meet the levy.'

sentences.zh: 」欲亡入匈奴。

translations.literal: Wished to flee into the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: They planned to bolt to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 戊己校尉刀護聞之，召置離驗問，辭服，乃械致都護但欽在所埒婁城。

translations.literal: Wu-Ji Colonel Dao Hu heard it, summoned Xuzhili to examine and question; he confessed submission; thereupon shackled and delivered him to Protector-General Dan Qin's place at Leilou city.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Dao Hu heard, questioned Xuzhili until he confessed, shackled him, and sent him to Dan Qin at Leilou.

sentences.zh: 置離人民知其不還，皆哭而送之。

translations.literal: Xuzhili's people knew he would not return, all wept and escorted him.
translations.idiomatic: His people knew he would not return and wept him on his way.

sentences.zh: 至，欽則斬置離。

translations.literal: When arrived, Qin then beheaded Xuzhili.
translations.idiomatic: Dan Qin executed him on arrival.

sentences.zh: 置離兄輔國侯狐蘭支將置離眾二千餘人，驅畜產，舉國亡降匈奴。

translations.literal: Xuzhili's elder brother the state-assisting marquis Hulanzhi led Xuzhili's host more than two thousand persons, drove livestock property, the whole state fled and surrendered to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: His brother Hulanzhi, assistant marquis, led over two thousand followers with herds and abandoned the kingdom for the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 是時，莽易單于璽，單于恨怒，遂受狐蘭支降，遣兵與共冠擊車師，殺後城長，傷都護司馬，及狐蘭兵復還入匈奴。

translations.literal: At this time Mang changed the Chanyu's seal; the Chanyu hated and was angry, thereupon accepted Hulanzhi's surrender, dispatched troops together with him to raid-strike Jushi, killed the rear city chief, wounded the Protector-General's major, and Hulanzhi's troops again returned into the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Mang had just humiliated the chanyu with a new seal; furious, he welcomed Hulanzhi, sent horse with him to raid Jushi, killed the rear-city chief, wounded the Protector-General's major, then pulled back into the steppe.

sentences.zh: 時戊己校尉刀護病，遣史陳良屯桓且谷備匈奴寇。

translations.literal: At the time Wu-Ji Colonel Dao Hu was ill, dispatched clerk Chen Liang to garrison Huanqie valley against Xiongnu raiding.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Dao Hu fell ill and sent clerk Chen Liang to hold Huanqie valley against Xiongnu raids.

sentences.zh: 史終帶取糧食，司馬丞韓玄領諸壁，右曲候任商領諸壘，相與謀曰：「西域諸國頗背叛，匈奴欲大侵。

translations.literal: Clerk Zhong Dai took grain supplies; major assistant Han Xuan commanded the ramparts; right qu-hou Ren Shang commanded the forts; together plotted, saying: 'Western Regions states largely rebel; the Xiongnu wish great invasion.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong Dai for supplies, Han Xuan held the walls, Ren Shang the redoubts; they schemed, 'The west is rising in revolt and the Xiongnu mean a major strike.

sentences.zh: 要死。

translations.literal: We are sure to die.
translations.idiomatic: We are dead men either way.

sentences.zh: 可殺校尉，將人眾降匈奴。

translations.literal: Can kill the colonel, lead the host to surrender to the Xiongnu.'
translations.idiomatic: Kill the colonel and lead everyone to the Xiongnu.'

sentences.zh: 」即將數千騎至校尉府，脅諸亭令燔積薪，分告諸壁曰：「匈奴十萬騎來人，吏士皆持兵，後者斬！

translations.literal: Thereupon led several thousand cavalry to the colonel's headquarters, coerced the watch-posts to burn piled firewood, separately told the ramparts: 'One hundred thousand Xiongnu cavalry are coming; officers and soldiers all take arms—who lags is beheaded!'
translations.idiomatic: They rode thousands strong to the colonel's yamen, forced beacons to burn their fuel piles, and cried along the walls, 'A hundred thousand Xiongnu are coming—arm every man; stragglers die!'

sentences.zh: 」得三四百人，去校尉府數里止，晨火然。

translations.literal: Obtained three or four hundred persons, went several li from the colonel's headquarters and halted; at dawn fire blazed.
translations.idiomatic: They gathered three or four hundred followers, halted a few li off, and at dawn lit great fires.

sentences.zh: 校尉開門擊鼓收吏士，良等隨人，遂殺校尉刀護及子男四人、諸昆弟子男，獨遺婦女小兒。

translations.literal: The colonel opened the gate, beat drums to gather officers and soldiers; Liang and others followed the men in, then killed Colonel Dao Hu and four sons, various brothers' sons, only leaving women and small children.
translations.idiomatic: The colonel opened gates and beat assembly; Chen Liang's men slipped in, slew Dao Hu and four sons and other male kin, sparing only women and children.

sentences.zh: 止留戊己校尉城，遣人與匈奴南將軍相聞，南將軍以二千騎迎良等。

translations.literal: Stopped holding Wu-Ji Colonel city, sent men to communicate with the Xiongnu South General; the South General with two thousand cavalry welcomed Liang and others.
translations.idiomatic: They held the Wu-Ji colonel's citadel, messaged the Xiongnu south general, who met them with two thousand horse.

sentences.zh: 良等盡脅略戊己校尉吏士男女二千餘人入匈奴。

translations.literal: Liang and others entirely coerced and plundered Wu-Ji Colonel clerks, soldiers, men and women more than two thousand persons into the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: They drove over two thousand colonel's men, women, and children into the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 單於以良、帶為烏賁都尉。

translations.literal: The Chanyu made Liang and Dai Wuben commandants.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu named them commandants of Wuben.

sentences.zh: 後三歲，單于死，弟烏累單于鹹立，復與莽和親。

translations.literal: After three years the Chanyu died; younger brother Wulie Chanyu Xian stood, again with Mang heqin.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later the chanyu died; his brother Xian, the Wulie chanyu, renewed peace with Mang.

sentences.zh: 莽遣使者多繼金幣賂單于，購求陳良、終帶等。

translations.literal: Mang dispatched envoys carrying much continued gold and silks to bribe the Chanyu, to purchase seeking Chen Liang, Zhong Dai and others.
translations.idiomatic: Mang sent envoys laden with gold and silk to buy back Chen Liang, Zhong Dai, and their confederates.

sentences.zh: 單于盡收四人及手殺刀護者芝音妻子以下二十七人，皆械檻車付使者。

translations.literal: The Chanyu entirely collected the four persons and the hand-killer of Dao Hu Zhiyin's wife and children down twenty-seven persons, all shackled in caged carts and handed to envoys.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu rounded up the four ringleaders, twenty-seven more including Zhiyin who had slain Dao Hu, and sent them in caged carts.

sentences.zh: 到長安，莽皆燒殺之。

translations.literal: Reaching Chang'an, Mang all burned and killed them.
translations.idiomatic: At Chang'an Mang burned them all alive.

sentences.zh: 其後莽復欺詐單于，和親遂絕。

translations.literal: Afterward Mang again deceived and cheated the Chanyu; heqin then broke off.
translations.idiomatic: Mang's further deceit ended the marriage alliance.

sentences.zh: 匈奴大擊北邊，而西域瓦解。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu greatly struck the northern border, while the Western Regions collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu hammered the northern frontier and the Western Regions fell apart.

sentences.zh: 焉耆國近匈奴，先叛，殺都護但欽，莽不能討。

translations.literal: Yanqi state was near the Xiongnu; first rebelled, killed Protector-General Dan Qin; Mang could not punish.
translations.idiomatic: Yanqi, close to the Xiongnu, revolted first, slew Protector-General Dan Qin, and Mang could not avenge him.

sentences.zh: 天鳳三年，乃遣五威將王駿、西域都護李崇將戊己校尉出西域，諸國皆郊迎，送兵谷，焉耆詐降而聚兵自備。

translations.literal: Tianfeng third year, then dispatched Five Might General Wang Jun and Western Regions Protector-General Li Chong leading Wu-Ji Colonel out the Western Regions; all states suburban-welcomed, sent troops and grain; Yanqi feigned surrender but gathered troops to prepare themselves.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Tianfeng, Wang Jun and Protector-General Li Chong led the Wu-Ji colonel west; states met them in the suburbs with grain and escorts, while Yanqi feigned submission and secretly armed.

sentences.zh: 駿等將莎車、龜慈兵七千餘人，分為數部入焉耆，焉耆伏兵要遮駿。

translations.literal: Jun and others led Kucha—here written Longci—troops more than seven thousand persons, divided into several columns entering Yanqi; Yanqi ambushed troops to intercept Jun.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Jun took over seven thousand troops from Khotan and Kucha in several columns into Yanqi, where ambushes waited.

sentences.zh: 及姑墨、尉犁、危須國兵為反間，還共襲擊駿等，皆殺之。

translations.literal: Together with Gumo, Weili, and Weixu troops acting as double agents, they returned jointly to raid-strike Jun and others, all killed them.
translations.idiomatic: Gumo, Weili, and Weixu troops turned coat and joined Yanqi in wiping out Wang Jun's force.

sentences.zh: 唯戊己校尉郭欽別將兵，後至焉耆。

translations.literal: Only Wu-Ji Colonel Guo Qin separately led troops, arriving later at Yanqi.
translations.idiomatic: Only Colonel Guo Qin came late with a separate column.

sentences.zh: 焉耆兵未還，欽擊殺其老弱，引兵還。

translations.literal: Yanqi troops had not yet returned; Qin struck and killed their old and weak, led troops back.
translations.idiomatic: Before Yanqi's army returned, Guo Qin cut down their noncombatants and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 莽封欽為剼鬍子。

translations.literal: Mang enfeoffed Qin as Marquis Who Shaves the Beard.
translations.idiomatic: Mang ennobled him as marquis of the shaved beard.

sentences.zh: 李崇收余士，還保龜茲。

translations.literal: Li Chong gathered remaining soldiers, returned and protected Qiuci.
translations.idiomatic: Li Chong rallied survivors and held Kucha.

sentences.zh: 數年莽死，崇遂沒，西域因絕。

translations.literal: Several years Mang died; Chong then perished; the Western Regions thereby broke off.
translations.idiomatic: Within a few years Mang died, Li Chong fell, and the Western Regions were severed from Han.

sentences.zh: 最凡國五十。

translations.literal: In all states fifty.
translations.idiomatic: In all there were fifty states.

sentences.zh: 自譯長、城長、君、監、吏、大祿、百工、千長、都尉、且渠、當戶、將、相至侯、王，皆佩漢印綬，凡三百七十六人。

translations.literal: From interpreter chief, city chief, lord, supervisor, clerk, Dalu, hundred crafts, thousand-chief, commandant, qiequ, danghu, general, minister down to marquis and king, all wore Han seals and cords; in all three hundred seventy-six persons.
translations.idiomatic: From interpreter chiefs up through kings, three hundred seventy-six western officers wore Han seals and ribbons.

sentences.zh: 而康居、大月氏、安息、罽賓、烏弋之屬，皆以絕遠不在數中，其來貢獻則相與報，不督錄總領也。

translations.literal: But Kangju, Great Yuezhi, Parthia, Jibin, Alexandria-Ecbatana types, all because cut off far are not counted within the number; when they come with tribute then mutually requite, not supervised, recorded, or overall led.
translations.idiomatic: Kangju, the Yuezhi, Parthia, Jibin, and the Indian states lay too far off to be tallied here; when they sent tribute Han answered in kind without listing or commanding them.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：孝武之世，圖制匈奴，患者兼從西國，結黨南羌，乃表河西，列四郡，開玉門，通四域，以斷匈奴右臂，隔絕南羌、月氏。

translations.literal: Praise says: In Emperor Xiaowu's age, planning to control the Xiongnu, the afflicted joined western states, formed faction with southern Qiang; thereupon displayed the Hexi, arrayed four commanderies, opened Jade Gate, penetrated the four regions, thereby to sever the Xiongnu's right arm, cut off southern Qiang and Yuezhi.
translations.idiomatic: The summation runs: Under Emperor Wu, scheming to master the Xiongnu, Han bound the afflicted western states and allied with the southern Qiang, marked out the Hexi, set four commanderies, opened the Jade Gate, and reached the four quarters to cut the Xiongnu right arm and wall off the southern Qiang and Yuezhi.

sentences.zh: 單于失援，由是遠遁，而幕南無王庭。

translations.literal: The Chanyu lost support; from this fled far, while south of the desert there was no royal court.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu lost his allies, fled deep into the steppe, and left no court south of the desert.

sentences.zh: 遭值文、景玄默，養民五世，天下殷富，財力有餘，士馬強盛。

translations.literal: Meeting the quiet depth of Wen and Jing, nurturing the people five generations, all under heaven was rich, wealth and strength had surplus, soldiers and horses were strong and flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven had enjoyed Wen and Jing's quiet rule for five generations, leaving the realm wealthy, the treasury full, and the army strong.

sentences.zh: 故能睹犀布、玳瑁則建珠崖七郡，感枸醬、竹杖則開牂柯、越巂，聞天馬、蒲陶則通大宛、安息。

translations.literal: Therefore able to see rhinoceros cloth and tortoise shell then establish Zhuya seven commanderies; moved by shell-ginger sauce and bamboo staffs then open Zangke and Yuexi; hearing of heavenly horses and grapes then open Dayuan and Parthia.
translations.idiomatic: Thus rhino hide and tortoise shell prompted the seven Zhuya commanderies; the famed sauce of Zangke and its bamboo staffs opened Zangke and Yuexi; tales of blood-sweat horses and grapes opened Ferghana and Parthia.

sentences.zh: 自是之後，明珠、文甲、通犀、翠羽之珍盈於後宮，薄梢、龍文、魚目、汗血之馬充於黃門，巨像、師子、猛犬、大雀之群食於外囿。

translations.literal: From this afterward bright pearls, patterned shells, through-going rhino, kingfisher plumes' treasures filled the rear palace; thin-shaft, dragon-pattern, fish-eye, blood-sweating horses filled the Yellow Gate; great elephants, lions, fierce dogs, great peacocks' flocks fed in the outer park.
translations.idiomatic: Thence pearls, tortoiseshell, rhino horn, and kingfisher feathers crowded the harem; fine horses filled the imperial stables; elephants, lions, mastiffs, and ostriches grazed the outer parks.

sentences.zh: 殊方異物，四面而至。

translations.literal: Strange lands' strange things, from four sides arrived.
translations.idiomatic: Curios from every quarter streamed to the throne.

sentences.zh: 於是廣開上林，穿昆明池，營千門萬戶之宮，立神明通天之台，興造甲乙之帳，落以隨珠和璧，天子負黼依，襲翠被，馮玉幾，而處其中。

translations.literal: Thereupon broadly opened Shanglin, dug Kunming pool, built palace of thousand gates and myriad doors, erected spirit-mingling-with-heaven tower, raised and made jia-yi tents, hung with Sui pearls and He disks; the Son of Heaven bore fu-yi screen, wore kingfisher coverlet, leaned on jade armrest, and dwelt within it.
translations.idiomatic: Then came the great expansion of the Shanglin Park, the cutting of Kunming Pool, palaces of a thousand gates, towers that seemed to touch the gods, paired canopies hung with legendary pearls and jade, and the Son of Heaven robed in embroidered screen and kingfisher quilt, resting on a jade armrest at the center of it all.

sentences.zh: 設酒池肉林以饗四夷之客，作《巴俞》都盧、海中《碭極》、漫衍魚龍、角抵之戲以觀視之。

translations.literal: Set wine pools and meat forests to feast the four Yi's guests; made Ba-Yu, Dulou, sea-inside Dangji, roaming-spread fish-dragon, horn-butting plays to observe and view them.
translations.idiomatic: Wine lakes and forests of meat entertained envoys from every quarter, while Ba and Yu acrobats, pole acts, Dangji sea music, roaming beast masques, and wrestling filled the eye.

sentences.zh: 及賂遺贈送，萬里相奉，師旅之費，不可勝計。

translations.literal: As to bribes, gifts, and presents, ten thousand li mutually offering; army hosts' expense, cannot be fully reckoned.
translations.idiomatic: Bribes and counter-gifts crossed ten thousand li, and the cost of armies defied reckoning.

sentences.zh: 至於用度不足，乃榷酒酤，管鹽鐵，鑄白金，造皮幣，算至車船，租及六畜。

translations.literal: Reaching to outlays insufficient, thereupon monopolized wine selling, managed salt and iron, cast white metal, made leather currency, calculated reaching carts and boats, rent extending to six livestock.
translations.idiomatic: When revenue failed, the state monopolized wine, took over salt and iron, minted token silver, issued leather notes, taxed carts and boats, and even levied rent on the six kinds of livestock.

sentences.zh: 民力屈，財力竭，因之以凶年，寇盜並起，道路不通，直指之使始出，衣繡杖斧，斷斬於郡國，然後勝之。

translations.literal: People's strength bent, wealth strength exhausted; following it with bad years; bandits and robbers together rose; roads not passable; straight-pointing envoys first went out, clothed in embroidery bearing axes, judged and beheaded in commanderies and kingdoms; afterward overcame them.
translations.idiomatic: The people were broken and the treasury drained; famine followed, banditry closed the roads, and only when axe-bearing commissioners in embroidered robes toured the provinces to judge and execute did order return.

sentences.zh: 是以末年遂棄輪台之地，而下哀痛之詔，豈非仁聖之所悔哉！

translations.literal: Therefore in the final years thereupon abandoned Luntai's lands, and issued downward a grief-stricken edict—is this not what benevolent sages regret!
translations.idiomatic: Hence at the close of the reign Luntai was abandoned and the sorrowful edict went forth—surely the very sort of regret a humane sage must feel.

sentences.zh: 且通西域，近有龍堆，遠則蔥嶺，身熱、頭痛、縣度之厄。

translations.literal: Moreover opening communication with the Western Regions: near there is Dragon Mound; far then Congling; body-heat, headache, Suspended Crossing's perils.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover the road west runs soon upon the White Dragon Mound, then far beyond to the Pamirs, with the fevers, the giddy heights, and the rope-bridge gorges in between.

sentences.zh: 淮南、杜欽、揚雄之論，皆以為此天地所以界別區域，絕外內也。

translations.literal: Huainan, Du Qin, and Yang Xiong's discourses all took this as that whereby Heaven and Earth demarcate regions and sever outer from inner.
translations.idiomatic: Writers from the Huainan circle to Du Qin and Yang Xiong have argued that these wastes are Heaven's way of marking off the world and dividing inner from outer lands.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰「西戎即序」，禹即就而序之，非上威服致其貢物也。

translations.literal: The Documents says 'Western Rong came to order'; Yu thereupon approached and ordered them—not by superior awe-submission causing their tribute goods.
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Documents says the western Rong 'were brought to order'—Yu arranged them by settlement, not by terrorizing them into tribute.

sentences.zh: 西域諸國，各有君長，兵眾分弱，無所統一，雖屬匈奴，不相親附。

translations.literal: The Western Regions various states each have rulers and chiefs; military hosts divided and weak; nothing unifies them; although belonging to the Xiongnu, they do not mutually draw close and attach.
translations.idiomatic: The oasis kingdoms each have their own lords; their forces are scattered and feeble, with no single hegemon, and though they acknowledge the Xiongnu they do not truly cleave to them.

sentences.zh: 匈奴能得其馬畜旃罽，而不能統率與之進退。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu can obtain their horses, livestock, felt, and rugs, but cannot command and lead them together in advance and retreat.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu may take their horses, herds, and felts, yet cannot march them as one obedient host.

sentences.zh: 與漢隔絕，道裡又遠，得之不為益，棄之不為損。

translations.literal: Cut off from Han, road mileage again far; obtaining them is not gain, abandoning them is not loss.
translations.idiomatic: They lie cut off from Han by immense distance; to win them adds little, to leave them costs little.

sentences.zh: 盛德在我，無取於彼。

translations.literal: Flourishing virtue is in us; nothing to take from them.
translations.idiomatic: Our own virtue is abundance enough; we need nothing from them.

sentences.zh: 故自建武以來，西域思漢威德，鹹樂內屬。

translations.literal: Therefore from Jianwu onward, the Western Regions longed for Han's awe and virtue; all were pleased to be inner subjects.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Jianwu era the western states have yearned for Han's majesty and grace, and one and all wish to enter the register as inner subjects.

sentences.zh: 唯其小邑鄯善、車師，界迫匈奴，尚為所拘。

translations.literal: Only their small towns Shanshan and Jushi, border pressed by the Xiongnu, still are by them constrained.
translations.idiomatic: Only the petty states of Shanshan and Jushi, squeezed against the Xiongnu frontier, remain under duress.

sentences.zh: 而其大國莎車、于闐之屬，數遣使置質於漢，願請屬都護。

translations.literal: But their great states such as Suoju and Yutian, repeatedly dispatched envoys setting hostages at Han, wished to request belonging to the Protector-General.
translations.idiomatic: The greater powers such as Yarkand and Khotan have sent envoys again and again, left hostages at court, and begged to be placed under the Protector-General.

sentences.zh: 聖上遠覽古今，因時之宜，羈縻不絕，辭而未許。

translations.literal: The sage sovereign far surveys past and present, according with the time's fitness; halter-rein unbroken; declined and not yet permitted.
translations.idiomatic: The sage emperor weighs past and present, fits policy to the hour, keeps the loose rein unbroken, yet has courteously declined—for the moment.

sentences.zh: 雖大禹之序西戎，周公之讓白雉，太宗之卻走馬，義兼之矣，亦何以尚茲！

translations.literal: Although Great Yu's ordering the western Rong, Duke of Zhou's yielding the white pheasant, Taizong's rejecting running horses—righteousness combined them—what could surpass this!
translations.idiomatic: Here is the righteousness that informed Yu's ordering of the western tribes, the Duke of Zhou's modest refusal of the white pheasant, and Emperor Taizong's return of the horses—combined in one policy; what could rank above it!
